page: Amazon Web Services r8i.24xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores based on the x86_64 architecture, utilizing Intel Xeon 6975P-C processors running at 3.9 GHz. The system is equipped with 768.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. It lacks local storage and dedicated GPU hardware, but includes a 40 Gbps baseline network interface.

Benchmark results indicate top-tier capabilities in memory bandwidth, with cached read-only small block speeds reaching 7675.4 GB/sec and uncached large block read speeds at 384.5 GB/sec. Multi-core processing is strong, as evidenced by a Passmark CPU score of 106946.29 and a multi-threaded bzip3 decompression rate of 657.04 GB/sec. The processor supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX), which contribute to top-tier performance in database operations and Redis SET operations.

The absence of local NVMe storage requires reliance on network-attached storage, which is a key architectural tradeoff. However, the high density of DDR5 memory and dedicated CPU allocation offer qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound workloads by reducing the need to scale horizontally. This server is optimized for memory-intensive applications, large-scale database operations, in-memory caching, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Amazon Web Services r8i.24xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores powered by Intel Xeon 6975P-C processors at 3.9 GHz. It is equipped with 768.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 7 GHz, delivering 8.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and provides a 40 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, though it lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmark data shows top-tier performance in memory bandwidth, database operations, and Redis SET operations, alongside strong multi-core CPU capabilities. The processor supports advanced vector and matrix extensions for accelerated mathematical computations. This configuration is designed for memory-intensive workloads, including large database deployments, in-memory data stores, and enterprise-scale data processing.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8i.24xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 96 vCPUs, 768 GB of DDR5 RAM, and Intel Xeon processors, delivering top-tier memory bandwidth and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r8i.24xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 96 vCPUs, 768 GB of DDR5 RAM, and Intel Xeon processors.
tagline: A memory-optimized platform featuring high-speed DDR5 RAM and advanced matrix extensions for demanding database and memory-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs and 768.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory
Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with AVX-512 and AMX extensions
Top-tier memory bandwidth and database operations performance
40 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the AWS Nitro hypervisor
Designed for in-memory databases, caching, and large-scale data processing
